steps.title: Identify the sentence structure
steps.content: The sentence has an independent clause—”no birder has ever achieved the ultimate goal” (where “goal” fills part of the blank)—followed by a phrase that elaborates on the goal. That phrase is the gerund form “catching an in-person glimpse of all 10,000-plus species of birds.”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: A colon can introduce a noun phrase or gerund phrase that elaborates on an independent clause. Choice C gives us “the ultimate goal: catching an in-person glimpse,” which shows what the goal is. The other choices violate the rule by either omitting punctuation (creating a grammatical error), inserting a period (creating a fragment), or using a semicolon (which requires independent clauses, not a gerund phrase). That matches choice C — C is correct.
